behavior_summary: This script implements a town guard NPC with: a simple 'U8P_GUARD2::look' description, proximity handlers to revive or re-position the guard and to start movement, a scheduler for periodic behaviour, and a full conversation handler that presents a question menu, gives directions, and updates persistent flags to avoid repeating information.
U8P_GUARD2::look: when the player inspects the NPC it barks either 'dead guardsman' or 'guardsman' depending on Npc::isDead(this).

U8P_GUARD2::enterFastArea: triggered when an actor enters the guard's fast-area. If the guard is dead it clears dead/combat state and teleports the NPC to specific coordinates; otherwise it resets an engine reference and invokes a local movement/patrol utility (U8P_GUARD2::func0C02).
U8P_GUARD2::leaveFastArea: cleans up by resetting the engine reference associated with the fast-area.
U8P_GUARD2::schedule: sets scheduling parameters, calls an engine register/start function, suspends waiting for the engine, and optionally runs a follow-up when the wait returns positive.

U8P_GUARD2::use: full dialogue flow. It spawns a short helper process, checks several titan/quest flags and may immediately warn the player if multiple titan-related flags are set, otherwise enters a menu loop (Who are you? What is your duty? Where am I? Bye) that removes asked questions, sets or clears persistent flags (guard2Met, guard2GaveDirections, guard2Pissed) and calls map/UI helpers to show directions.

U8P_GUARD2::func0C02: local utility implementing an alternating pathfind/patrol sequence that repeatedly launches Npc::pathfind to a waypoint, waits on spawned processes, calls a map/movement helper, and toggles a local state for the next cycle.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GUARD2::look -> if Npc::isDead(this) then bark 'dead guardsman' else bark 'guardsman' (suspend after bark).
U8P_GUARD2::enterFastArea -> if Npc::isDead(this) then Npc::clrDead/ClrInCombat + Npc::teleport(...) else FREE::resetRef(0x0205, this) + call U8P_GUARD2::func0C02.

U8P_GUARD2::use -> spawn helper (FREE::func1C19) and FREE::resetRef, count titan-related flags (hydrosFree, hasHeart, pyrosFree); if count >= threshold then immediate warning bark and end; otherwise present menu loop, handle choices by removing options, setting/clearing guard flags, and calling map/UI helpers (METHOD::func091C, METHOD::func15E7) for directions.
U8P_GUARD2::schedule -> set local scheduling constants, call METHOD::func143A to register/start, suspend and on process_result() true call METHOD::func133F then suspend again.

U8P_GUARD2::func0C02 -> alternating branch on local1 toggle: launch Npc::pathfind(0x0A, ...), suspend/wait on pid, spawn FREE::func1C19 twice with waits, call METHOD::func091C, then flip local1 to alternate next invocation.
open_questions: What exactly do METHOD::func091C and METHOD::func15E7 do for the player UI? (inferred to be map/waypoint helpers; exact UI effects unknown).

What are the semantic meanings of the numeric constants used in U8P_GUARD2::schedule (0x28, 0x331F, 0x37FF, 0x30) and the gating constant 0x2D in U8P_GUARD2::func0C02? (likely engine/scheduler or timing parameters).
What precise engine bookkeeping is performed by FREE::resetRef and what do the numeric reference ids (0x0205, 0x0200) map to? (inferred to be fast-area or script references).
What side-effects or animations are produced by the spawned helper FREE::func1C19 used in dialogue and U8P_GUARD2::func0C02? (inferred to be timing/animation helpers).
Is hasHeart a player inventory/quest flag (inferred) or something else in the world state?
Are there additional external systems that toggle guard2Pissed outside this script (how is it set initially)?

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD2::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short examine/U8P_GUARD2::look description for the guard NPC, differing if the NPC is dead or alive.
entry_conditions: Triggered when the player 'looks at' or examines the NPC (engine U8P_GUARD2::look/inspect event).
Immediate branch on Npc::isDead(this) to choose between the 'dead guardsman' and 'guardsman' barks.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD2::enterFastArea
function_map.role: proximity trigger — revive/teleport dead guard or reset refs and start movement

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD2::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: proximity exit cleanup — reset engine reference for fast-area

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD2::schedule
function_map.role: set up and register scheduled behaviour / wait for scheduler events

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD2::use
function_map.role: conversation/dialogue handler presenting menu, giving directions and mutating persistent flags

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD2::func0C02
function_map.role: utility for alternating pathfinding/patrol sequence (movement loop)

internal_calls.purpose: When guard is alive, start the internal movement/patrol utility to position the NPC or begin pathfinding.

external_calls.purpose: Check whether the NPC is dead to select the appropriate bark.
Decide between revive/teleport branch and normal start-of-activity branch.
Clear dead flag (revive) when area entry finds the NPC dead.
Clear in-combat status after reviving so NPC is not hostile immediately.
Reposition the revived NPC to specific coordinates.
Reset engine/bookkeeping reference for the NPC when starting normal behavior.
Cleanup/reset the fast-area engine reference when an actor leaves the trigger area.
Spawn a short-lived helper/process used as a timing or animation synchronization point before and during dialogue.
Reset engine reference early in the dialogue flow (bookkeeping of fast-area or script refs).
Show or highlight directions/route on the player's map or UI while giving location directions.
Complementary UI/map helper used with func091C when presenting directions (e.g., waypoint, label or camera adjustment).
Register or begin scheduled behaviour with the engine (pass configured local parameters).
Follow-up action invoked when a suspended U8P_GUARD2::schedule wait returns a positive process_result().
Start an asynchronous pathfinding process for the NPC to move to a waypoint (patrol/movement).
Spawn helper processes used as timing/sequence points during the pathfind/patrol loop.
Perform a map/movement related call during the movement sequence (likely visual or registration step).

reads.name: hydrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to a titan-alert counter during dialogue; presence increments a local accumulator used to trigger an immediate 'Titans are angry' warning.

reads.name: pyrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to the same titan-alert counter used by the dialogue pre-check.

reads.name: hasHeart
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to the titan-alert counter and modifies an early dialogue branch; likely a quest/inventory flag.

reads.name: guard2Met
reads.likely_effect: Read to choose greeting variants (different replies if the player has previously met this guard).

reads.name: guard2Pissed
reads.likely_effect: Read to choose an annoyed terse greeting; when observed it is cleared after the annoyed line.

reads.name: guard2GaveDirections
reads.likely_effect: Read to decide whether to repeat detailed directions or give a short admonition instead.

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Used to choose which named NPC the guard references when answering 'What is your duty?'.

reads.name: benticMet
reads.likely_effect: Alters location advice in directions if the player has met Bentic.

writes.name: guard2Met
writes.likely_effect: Set when player asks 'Who are you?' to record that the guard has met the player and avoid repeating that dialog branch.

writes.name: guard2Pissed
writes.likely_effect: Cleared after delivering an annoyed line to reset the guard's annoyed state for future interactions.

writes.name: guard2GaveDirections
writes.likely_effect: Set after giving directions to avoid repeating long directions on subsequent interactions.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a town guard NPC with: a simple 'U8P_GUARD2::look' description, proximity handlers to revive or re-position the guard and to start movement, a scheduler for periodic behaviour, and a full conversation handler that presents a question menu, gives directions, and updates persistent flags to avoid repeating information.
